title: Open-source AI predicts key peptide traits before costly lab testing
excerpt: Penn Engineers have developed PeptiVerse, an AI-powered platform that predicts key chemical and biological properties of peptides, strings of amino acids whose medical potential has been demonstrated by the success of GLP-1 drugs, widely used weight-loss treatments.
content_html:
Penn Engineers have developed PeptiVerse, an AI-powered platform that predicts key chemical and biological properties of peptides, strings of amino acids whose medical potential has been demonstrated by the success of GLP-1 drugs, widely used weight-loss treatments.

In an article published in Nature Communications, the researchers describe how they trained PeptiVerse using a wide range of data sets, allowing it to predict properties that can help determine whether a peptide is worth pursuing as a potential drug, such as the peptide's likelihood of dissolving, entering cells, avoiding toxicity and lasting long enough in the body to have an effect.

While tools for predicting such properties exist, those tools often focus on a narrower set of traits or only one kind of peptide. PeptiVerse, by contrast, brings many of those predictions together in one open-source, easily accessible platform, allowing users to evaluate both ordinary peptides and chemically modified versions designed to work better as drugs.

"Peptide drugs have enormous potential, but binding to the right target is only one part of what makes a molecule useful," says Pranam Chatterjee, Africk-Lesley Distinguished Scholar of Innovation in Engineering and assistant professor of bioengineering (BE) and computer and information science (CIS), and senior author of the new study.

"In drug discovery, one of the worst outcomes is finding out too late that a promising molecule cannot actually become a medicine," says Chatterjee. "PeptiVerse gives researchers a way to check many of those make-or-break properties earlier, before they invest the time and resources required to synthesize and test candidate drugs."

Putting peptide predictions in one place

To build PeptiVerse, the researchers first had to gather data that had been scattered across separate studies. Some data sets described whether peptides dissolve easily. Others measured whether peptides enter cells, avoid damaging red blood cells, resist unwanted protein buildup or remain active over time.

Synthesizing the data sets required far more than saving them in the same location. "Each data set came from a different type of experiment," says Sophia Vincoff, a doctoral student in BE and co-author of the study. "We had to understand what each experiment was measuring, standardize the data for each property and organize it so machine-learning models could learn from it and be tested fairly. Then we compared many model architectures to find the best approach for each prediction task."

Rather than assuming that one model works best for every task, PeptiVerse uses the strongest-performing model for each property. In some cases, simpler models performed as well as or better than more sophisticated approaches. In others, the opposite was true.

"We wanted PeptiVerse to function like a toolkit," says Yinuo Zhang, a postdoctoral researcher in BE and the paper's first author. "Instead of having each predictor live separately, PeptiVerse puts them together in a platform that can grow as new data and models become available."

Making peptide predictions easier to use

Most new computational tools come in the form of libraries or packages, bundles of code that researchers can download and run on their own computers but that typically require some level of facility with programming to operate.

PeptiVerse, by contrast, includes a web interface that allows users to simply type in a peptide sequence, select properties and receive predictions through a visual dashboard.

That design makes the platform especially useful for experimental researchers, like biologists, who may have peptide candidates they are already studying and want a fast way to assess whether those molecules are likely to have the properties needed for further testing.

"I come from a biology perspective, and I'm a very visual person," says Zhang. "We wanted researchers to be able to see what was going on, not just download a Python package. The interface makes PeptiVerse something people can interact with directly."

The platform also lets users view the data used to train the models, helping them understand how their own peptide candidates compare with molecules that have already been experimentally characterized.

"For experimentalists, it matters where the data came from," says Vincoff. "PeptiVerse makes it easier to look at the data behind the predictions, which helps users interpret what the models are telling them."

From screening to generating drug candidates

One immediate use of PeptiVerse is as a screening tool. Rather than synthesizing and testing peptide candidates one by one, researchers in academia and industry alike can use the platform to quickly assess which molecules appear most promising before moving into the lab.

The platform could also play a more ambitious role in AI-driven drug discovery. In addition to evaluating existing candidates, PeptiVerse can be paired with generative AI tools that propose entirely new peptides. That approach has already helped the Chatterjee Lab develop new peptide-design innovations, including PepTune, TR2-D2, MOG-DFM and moPPIt, which use property predictions from PeptiVerse to guide the design of peptides with desired therapeutic features.

In this paradigm, rather than serving only as a filter to screen existing peptides, PeptiVerse can shape the search for new drug candidates. The system's powerful predictions allow generative AI models to prioritize candidates with desirable characteristics—such as stronger binding, reduced nonspecific interactions with other proteins, better solubility, greater permeability and lower toxicity—before selecting particular molecules for synthesis and real-world testing.

"The property predictions become part of the search itself," says Chatterjee. "Instead of evaluating peptides only after they are generated, we can use those predictions to guide generative AI models toward molecules with the characteristics we want from the very beginning."

An expanding universe of peptide data

The researchers also designed PeptiVerse to keep growing. As more peptide data becomes available, the platform can be updated with new data sets, improved models and additional properties. That matters because some peptide properties are still harder to predict than others, largely because there is less experimental data available to train models about them.

More measurements could help PeptiVerse improve existing predictions and add new ones, such as whether a peptide is likely to activate or block a receptor and thereby activate or shut down a particular biochemical pathway. "PeptiVerse is not meant to be finished," says Chatterjee. "The more data the community contributes, the better these models can become."

Since PeptiVerse is open source, researchers can adapt it for their own purposes. Academic labs can use the existing platform, contribute data or build new predictors. Companies developing peptide therapeutics could also use the framework with their own internal data sets, creating versions of PeptiVerse tailored to the kinds of molecules they are trying to develop.

"The universe of possible peptides is too large for any one lab to map on its own," says Chatterjee. "We built PeptiVerse so that other researchers can help expand the map, adding new data and models that accelerate the search for new and better peptide drugs."

Publication details

Yinuo Zhang et al, PeptiVerse: A unified platform for therapeutic peptide property prediction, Nature Communications (2026). DOI: 10.1038/s41467-026-74167-w

Who's behind this story?

Lisa Lock

BA art history, MA material culture. Former museum editor, paramedic, and transplant coordinator. Editing for Science X since 2021. Full profile →

Robert Egan

Bachelor's in mathematical biology, Master's in creative writing. Well-traveled with unique perspectives on science and language. Full profile →

content_text: A laptop running PeptiVerse, at left, next to machines used to synthesize peptides, center and at right. Researchers can use PeptiVerse to predict the properties of peptides before incurring the time and expense required to synthesize them. Credit: Sylvia Zhang, Penn Engineering Penn Engineers have developed PeptiVerse, an AI-powered platform that predicts key chemical and biological properties of peptides, strings of amino acids whose medical potential has been demonstrated by the success of GLP-1 drugs, widely used weight-loss treatments. In an article published in Nature Communications, the researchers describe how they trained PeptiVerse using a wide range of data sets, allowing it to predict properties that can help determine whether a peptide is worth pursuing as a potential drug, such as the peptide's likelihood of dissolving, entering cells, avoiding toxicity and lasting long enough in the body to have an effect. While tools for predicting such properties exist, those tools often focus on a narrower set of traits or only one kind of peptide. "PeptiVerse gives researchers a way to check many of those make-or-break properties earlier, before they invest the time and resources required to synthesize and test candidate drugs." Members of the Chatterjee Lab—Elizabeth Mahood, at right, and Yesol Kim, at left—demonstrate how PeptiVerse, whose web client is open on the laptop, can be used by experimentalists to make predictions about peptides, which can then be synthesized by a machine like the one at right. "I come from a biology perspective, and I'm a very visual person," says Zhang. "We wanted researchers to be able to see what was going on, not just download a Python package. The interface makes PeptiVerse something people can interact with directly." The platform also lets users view the data used to train the models, helping them understand how their own peptide candidates compare with molecules that have already been experimentally characterized. "For experimentalists, it matters where the data came from," says Vincoff. "PeptiVerse makes it easier to look at the data behind the predictions, which helps users interpret what the models are telling them." Built from a wide range of data, PeptiVerse can predict multiple peptide properties, helping guide both AI generation of new peptides and choosing which peptides to synthesize in the lab. Credit: Chatterjee Lab, Penn From screening to generating drug candidates One immediate use of PeptiVerse is as a screening tool. Rather than synthesizing and testing peptide candidates one by one, researchers in academia and industry alike can use the platform to quickly assess which molecules appear most promising before moving into the lab. <figcaption>

sentences.en: A laptop running PeptiVerse, at left, next to machines used to synthesize peptides, center and at right.
sentences.zh: 左边是一台运行 PeptiVerse 的笔记本电脑，右边是用于合成肽的机器。

sentences.en: Researchers can use PeptiVerse to predict the properties of peptides before incurring the time and expense required to synthesize them.
sentences.zh: 研究人员可以使用 PeptiVerse 来预测肽的特性，然后再花费合成所需的时间和费用。

sentences.en: Credit: Sylvia Zhang, Penn Engineering
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Sylvia 张，宾夕法尼亚大学工程学院

blocks.html_en: Penn Engineers have developed PeptiVerse, an AI-powered platform that predicts key chemical and biological properties of peptides, strings of amino acids whose medical potential has been demonstrated by the success of GLP-1 drugs, widely used weight-loss treatments.

sentences.en: Penn Engineers have developed PeptiVerse, an AI-powered platform that predicts key chemical and biological properties of peptides, strings of amino acids whose medical potential has been demonstrated by the success of GLP-1 drugs, widely used weight-loss treatments.
sentences.zh: Penn Engineers 开发了 PeptiVerse，这是一个人工智能驱动的平台，可以预测肽和氨基酸串的关键化学和生物特性，GLP-1 药物和广泛使用的减肥疗法的成功证明了其医疗潜力。

blocks.html_en: In an article published in Nature Communications, the researchers describe how they trained PeptiVerse using a wide range of data sets, allowing it to predict properties that can help determine whether a peptide is worth pursuing as a potential drug, such as the peptide's likelihood of dissolving, entering cells, avoiding toxicity and lasting long enough in the body to have an effect.

sentences.en: In an article published in Nature Communications, the researchers describe how they trained PeptiVerse using a wide range of data sets, allowing it to predict properties that can help determine whether a peptide is worth pursuing as a potential drug, such as the peptide's likelihood of dissolving, entering cells, avoiding toxicity and lasting long enough in the body to have an effect.
sentences.zh: 在《自然通讯》上发表的一篇文章中，研究人员描述了他们如何使用广泛的数据集训练 PeptiVerse，使其能够预测有助于确定肽是否值得作为潜在药物的特性，例如肽溶解、进入细胞、避免毒性以及在体内持续足够长的时间以产生作用的可能性。

blocks.html_en: While tools for predicting such properties exist, those tools often focus on a narrower set of traits or only one kind of peptide. PeptiVerse, by contrast, brings many of those predictions together in one open-source, easily accessible platform, allowing users to evaluate both ordinary peptides and chemically modified versions designed to work better as drugs.

sentences.en: While tools for predicting such properties exist, those tools often focus on a narrower set of traits or only one kind of peptide.
sentences.zh: 虽然存在预测此类特性的工具，但这些工具通常关注一组较窄的特征或仅关注一种肽。

sentences.en: PeptiVerse, by contrast, brings many of those predictions together in one open-source, easily accessible platform, allowing users to evaluate both ordinary peptides and chemically modified versions designed to work better as drugs.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，PeptiVerse 将许多这样的预测整合到一个开源、易于访问的平台中，使用户能够评估普通肽和旨在更好地作为药物发挥作用的化学修饰版本。

blocks.html_en: "Peptide drugs have enormous potential, but binding to the right target is only one part of what makes a molecule useful," says Pranam Chatterjee, Africk-Lesley Distinguished Scholar of Innovation in Engineering and assistant professor of bioengineering (BE) and computer and information science (CIS), and senior author of the new study.

sentences.en: "Peptide drugs have enormous potential, but binding to the right target is only one part of what makes a molecule useful," says Pranam Chatterjee, Africk-Lesley Distinguished Scholar of Innovation in Engineering and assistant professor of bioengineering (BE) and computer and information science (CIS), and senior author of the new study.
sentences.zh: “肽药物具有巨大的潜力，但与正确的靶点结合只是分子发挥作用的一部分，”Africk-Lesley 杰出工程创新学者、生物工程 (BE) 和计算机与信息科学 (CIS) 助理教授、这项新研究的资深作者 Pranam Chatterjee 说。

blocks.html_en: "In drug discovery, one of the worst outcomes is finding out too late that a promising molecule cannot actually become a medicine," says Chatterjee. "PeptiVerse gives researchers a way to check many of those make-or-break properties earlier, before they invest the time and resources required to synthesize and test candidate drugs."

sentences.en: "In drug discovery, one of the worst outcomes is finding out too late that a promising molecule cannot actually become a medicine," says Chatterjee.
sentences.zh: 查特吉说：“在药物发现过程中，最糟糕的结果之一是发现为时已晚，一种有前途的分子实际上无法成为一种药物。”

sentences.en: "PeptiVerse gives researchers a way to check many of those make-or-break properties earlier, before they invest the time and resources required to synthesize and test candidate drugs."
sentences.zh: “PeptiVerse 为研究人员提供了一种方法，可以在投入合成和测试候选药物所需的时间和资源之前更早地检查许多这些成败的特性。”

sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，更简单的模型比更复杂的方法表现得更好，甚至更好。

sentences.zh: 在其他情况下，情况恰恰相反。

blocks.html_en: The platform also lets users view the data used to train the models, helping them understand how their own peptide candidates compare with molecules that have already been experimentally characterized.

sentences.en: The platform also lets users view the data used to train the models, helping them understand how their own peptide candidates compare with molecules that have already been experimentally characterized.
sentences.zh: 该平台还允许用户查看用于训练模型的数据，帮助他们了解自己的候选肽与已经经过实验表征的分子的比较。

blocks.html_en: "For experimentalists, it matters where the data came from," says Vincoff. "PeptiVerse makes it easier to look at the data behind the predictions, which helps users interpret what the models are telling them."

sentences.en: "For experimentalists, it matters where the data came from," says Vincoff.
sentences.zh: “对于实验者来说，数据来自哪里很重要，”文科夫说。

sentences.en: "PeptiVerse makes it easier to look at the data behind the predictions, which helps users interpret what the models are telling them."
sentences.zh: “PeptiVerse 使查看预测背后的数据变得更加容易，这有助于用户解释模型告诉他们的内容。”

blocks.html_en:
<figcaption>
                Built from a wide range of data, PeptiVerse can predict multiple peptide properties, helping guide both AI generation of new peptides and choosing which peptides to synthesize in the lab. Credit: Chatterjee Lab, Penn
            </figcaption>

sentences.en: Built from a wide range of data, PeptiVerse can predict multiple peptide properties, helping guide both AI generation of new peptides and choosing which peptides to synthesize in the lab.
sentences.zh: PeptiVerse 基于广泛的数据，可以预测多种肽特性，帮助指导人工智能生成新肽以及选择在实验室合成哪些肽。

sentences.en: Credit: Chatterjee Lab, Penn
sentences.zh: 图片来源：宾夕法尼亚大学查特吉实验室

blocks.html_en: From screening to generating drug candidates

sentences.en: From screening to generating drug candidates
sentences.zh: 从筛选到产生候选药物

blocks.html_en: One immediate use of PeptiVerse is as a screening tool. Rather than synthesizing and testing peptide candidates one by one, researchers in academia and industry alike can use the platform to quickly assess which molecules appear most promising before moving into the lab.

sentences.en: One immediate use of PeptiVerse is as a screening tool.
sentences.zh: PeptiVerse 的一个直接用途是作为筛选工具。

sentences.en: Rather than synthesizing and testing peptide candidates one by one, researchers in academia and industry alike can use the platform to quickly assess which molecules appear most promising before moving into the lab.
sentences.zh: 学术界和工业界的研究人员可以使用该平台在进入实验室之前快速评估哪些分子最有前途，而不是一一合成和测试候选肽。

blocks.html_en: The platform could also play a more ambitious role in AI-driven drug discovery. In addition to evaluating existing candidates, PeptiVerse can be paired with generative AI tools that propose entirely new peptides. That approach has already helped the Chatterjee Lab develop new peptide-design innovations, including PepTune, TR2-D2, MOG-DFM and moPPIt, which use property predictions from PeptiVerse to guide the design of peptides with desired therapeutic features.

sentences.en: The platform could also play a more ambitious role in AI-driven drug discovery.
sentences.zh: 该平台还可以在人工智能驱动的药物发现中发挥更雄心勃勃的作用。

sentences.en: In addition to evaluating existing candidates, PeptiVerse can be paired with generative AI tools that propose entirely new peptides.
sentences.zh: 除了评估现有候选肽之外，PeptiVerse 还可以与提出全新肽的生成式人工智能工具配合使用。

sentences.en: That approach has already helped the Chatterjee Lab develop new peptide-design innovations, including PepTune, TR2-D2, MOG-DFM and moPPIt, which use property predictions from PeptiVerse to guide the design of peptides with desired therapeutic features.
sentences.zh: 这种方法已经帮助 Chatterjee 实验室开发了新的肽设计创新，包括 PepTune、TR2-D2、MOG-DFM 和 moPPIt，它们使用 PeptiVerse 的特性预测来指导具有所需治疗功能的肽的设计。

blocks.html_en: In this paradigm, rather than serving only as a filter to screen existing peptides, PeptiVerse can shape the search for new drug candidates. The system's powerful predictions allow generative AI models to prioritize candidates with desirable characteristics—such as stronger binding, reduced nonspecific interactions with other proteins, better solubility, greater permeability and lower toxicity—before selecting particular molecules for synthesis and real-world testing.

sentences.en: In this paradigm, rather than serving only as a filter to screen existing peptides, PeptiVerse can shape the search for new drug candidates.
sentences.zh: 在此范例中，PeptiVerse 不仅仅充当筛选现有肽的过滤器，还可以塑造新候选药物的搜索。

sentences.en: The system's powerful predictions allow generative AI models to prioritize candidates with desirable characteristics—such as stronger binding, reduced nonspecific interactions with other proteins, better solubility, greater permeability and lower toxicity—before selecting particular molecules for synthesis and real-world testing.
sentences.zh: 该系统强大的预测功能使生成式人工智能模型能够优先考虑具有所需特征的候选分子，例如更强的结合力、减少与其他蛋白质的非特异性相互作用、更好的溶解度、更大的渗透性和更低的毒性，然后再选择特定的分子进行合成和实际测试。

blocks.html_en: "The property predictions become part of the search itself," says Chatterjee. "Instead of evaluating peptides only after they are generated, we can use those predictions to guide generative AI models toward molecules with the characteristics we want from the very beginning."

sentences.en: "The property predictions become part of the search itself," says Chatterjee.
sentences.zh: “财产预测成为搜索本身的一部分，”查特吉说。

sentences.en: "Instead of evaluating peptides only after they are generated, we can use those predictions to guide generative AI models toward molecules with the characteristics we want from the very beginning."
sentences.zh: “我们可以使用这些预测来指导生成式人工智能模型从一开始就具有我们想要的特征的分子，而不是仅在生成肽后对其进行评估。”

blocks.html_en: An expanding universe of peptide data

sentences.en: An expanding universe of peptide data
sentences.zh: 不断扩大的肽数据领域

blocks.html_en: The researchers also designed PeptiVerse to keep growing. As more peptide data becomes available, the platform can be updated with new data sets, improved models and additional properties. That matters because some peptide properties are still harder to predict than others, largely because there is less experimental data available to train models about them.

sentences.en: The researchers also designed PeptiVerse to keep growing.
sentences.zh: 研究人员还设计了 PeptiVerse 以保持增长。

sentences.en: As more peptide data becomes available, the platform can be updated with new data sets, improved models and additional properties.
sentences.zh: 随着更多的肽数据可用，该平台可以使用新的数据集、改进的模型和其他特性进行更新。

sentences.en: That matters because some peptide properties are still harder to predict than others, largely because there is less experimental data available to train models about them.
sentences.zh: 这很重要，因为某些肽特性仍然比其他特性更难预测，很大程度上是因为可用于训练有关它们的模型的实验数据较少。

blocks.html_en: More measurements could help PeptiVerse improve existing predictions and add new ones, such as whether a peptide is likely to activate or block a receptor and thereby activate or shut down a particular biochemical pathway. "PeptiVerse is not meant to be finished," says Chatterjee. "The more data the community contributes, the better these models can become."

sentences.en: More measurements could help PeptiVerse improve existing predictions and add new ones, such as whether a peptide is likely to activate or block a receptor and thereby activate or shut down a particular biochemical pathway.
sentences.zh: 更多的测量可以帮助 PeptiVerse 改进现有的预测并添加新的预测，例如肽是否可能激活或阻断受体，从而激活或关闭特定的生化途径。

sentences.en: "PeptiVerse is not meant to be finished," says Chatterjee.
sentences.zh: “PeptiVerse 并不意味着已经完成，”Chatterjee 说道。

sentences.en: "The more data the community contributes, the better these models can become."
sentences.zh: “社区贡献的数据越多，这些模型就会变得越好。”

blocks.html_en: Since PeptiVerse is open source, researchers can adapt it for their own purposes. Academic labs can use the existing platform, contribute data or build new predictors. Companies developing peptide therapeutics could also use the framework with their own internal data sets, creating versions of PeptiVerse tailored to the kinds of molecules they are trying to develop.

sentences.en: Since PeptiVerse is open source, researchers can adapt it for their own purposes.
sentences.zh: 由于 PeptiVerse 是开源的，研究人员可以根据自己的目的对其进行调整。

sentences.en: Academic labs can use the existing platform, contribute data or build new predictors.
sentences.zh: 学术实验室可以使用现有平台，贡献数据或构建新的预测器。

sentences.en: Companies developing peptide therapeutics could also use the framework with their own internal data sets, creating versions of PeptiVerse tailored to the kinds of molecules they are trying to develop.
sentences.zh: 开发肽疗法的公司还可以使用该框架与自己的内部数据集，创建适合他们试图开发的分子类型的 PeptiVerse 版本。

blocks.html_en: "The universe of possible peptides is too large for any one lab to map on its own," says Chatterjee. "We built PeptiVerse so that other researchers can help expand the map, adding new data and models that accelerate the search for new and better peptide drugs."

sentences.en: "The universe of possible peptides is too large for any one lab to map on its own," says Chatterjee.
sentences.zh: “可能的肽宇宙太大了，任何一个实验室都无法独自绘制地图，”查特吉说。

sentences.en: "We built PeptiVerse so that other researchers can help expand the map, adding new data and models that accelerate the search for new and better peptide drugs."
sentences.zh: “我们构建了 PeptiVerse，以便其他研究人员可以帮助扩展地图，添加新的数据和模型，从而加速寻找新的、更好的肽药物。”

content_html_zh:
Penn Engineers 开发了 PeptiVerse，这是一个人工智能驱动的平台，可以预测肽和氨基酸串的关键化学和生物特性，GLP-1 药物和广泛使用的减肥疗法的成功证明了其医疗潜力。

在《自然通讯》上发表的一篇文章中，研究人员描述了他们如何使用广泛的数据集训练 PeptiVerse，使其能够预测有助于确定肽是否值得作为潜在药物的特性，例如肽溶解、进入细胞、避免毒性以及在体内持续足够长的时间以产生作用的可能性。

虽然存在预测此类特性的工具，但这些工具通常关注一组较窄的特征或仅关注一种肽。 相比之下，PeptiVerse 将许多这样的预测整合到一个开源、易于访问的平台中，使用户能够评估普通肽和旨在更好地作为药物发挥作用的化学修饰版本。

“肽药物具有巨大的潜力，但与正确的靶点结合只是分子发挥作用的一部分，”Africk-Lesley 杰出工程创新学者、生物工程 (BE) 和计算机与信息科学 (CIS) 助理教授、这项新研究的资深作者 Pranam Chatterjee 说。

查特吉说：“在药物发现过程中，最糟糕的结果之一是发现为时已晚，一种有前途的分子实际上无法成为一种药物。” “PeptiVerse 为研究人员提供了一种方法，可以在投入合成和测试候选药物所需的时间和资源之前更早地检查许多这些成败的特性。”

将肽预测放在一处

为了构建 PeptiVerse，研究人员首先必须收集分散在不同研究中的数据。 一些数据集描述了肽是否容易溶解。 其他人则测量肽是否进入细胞、避免损害红细胞、抵抗不需要的蛋白质积聚或随着时间的推移保持活性。

合成数据集所需的不仅仅是将它们保存在同一位置。 “每个数据集都来自不同类型的实验，”BE 博士生、该研究的合著者 Sophia Vincoff 说。 “我们必须了解每个实验测量的内容，标准化每个属性的数据并对其进行组织，以便机器学习模型可以从中学习并公平地进行测试。 然后我们比较了许多模型架构，以找到每个预测任务的最佳方法。”

PeptiVerse 并没有假设一个模型最适合每项任务，而是针对每项属性使用性能最强的模型。 在某些情况下，更简单的模型比更复杂的方法表现得更好，甚至更好。 在其他情况下，情况恰恰相反。

“我们希望 PeptiVerse 能够像一个工具包一样发挥作用，”BE 博士后研究员、该论文的第一作者 Yino Zhang 说道。 “PeptiVerse 不是让每个预测器单独存在，而是将它们放在一个平台中，该平台可以随着新数据和模型的出现而不断增长。”

使肽预测更易于使用

大多数新的计算工具都以库或包、代码包的形式出现，研究人员可以下载这些代码并在自己的计算机上运行，​​但通常需要一定程度的编程设施才能操作。

相比之下，PeptiVerse 包括一个网络界面，允许用户简单地输入肽序列、选择属性并通过可视化仪表板接收预测。

这种设计使得该平台对于生物学家等实验研究人员特别有用，他们可能已经在研究候选肽，并希望有一种快速的方法来评估这些分子是否可能具有进一步测试所需的特性。

“我是从生物学的角度出发的，而且我是一个非常有视觉感的人，”张说。 “我们希望研究人员能够看到正在发生的事情，而不仅仅是下载 Python 包。 该界面使 PeptiVerse 成为人们可以直接交互的东西。”

该平台还允许用户查看用于训练模型的数据，帮助他们了解自己的候选肽与已经经过实验表征的分子的比较。

“对于实验者来说，数据来自哪里很重要，”文科夫说。 “PeptiVerse 使查看预测背后的数据变得更加容易，这有助于用户解释模型告诉他们的内容。”

从筛选到产生候选药物

PeptiVerse 的一个直接用途是作为筛选工具。 学术界和工业界的研究人员可以使用该平台在进入实验室之前快速评估哪些分子最有前途，而不是一一合成和测试候选肽。

该平台还可以在人工智能驱动的药物发现中发挥更雄心勃勃的作用。 除了评估现有候选肽之外，PeptiVerse 还可以与提出全新肽的生成式人工智能工具配合使用。 这种方法已经帮助 Chatterjee 实验室开发了新的肽设计创新，包括 PepTune、TR2-D2、MOG-DFM 和 moPPIt，它们使用 PeptiVerse 的特性预测来指导具有所需治疗功能的肽的设计。

在此范例中，PeptiVerse 不仅仅充当筛选现有肽的过滤器，还可以塑造新候选药物的搜索。 该系统强大的预测功能使生成式人工智能模型能够优先考虑具有所需特征的候选分子，例如更强的结合力、减少与其他蛋白质的非特异性相互作用、更好的溶解度、更大的渗透性和更低的毒性，然后再选择特定的分子进行合成和实际测试。

“财产预测成为搜索本身的一部分，”查特吉说。 “我们可以使用这些预测来指导生成式人工智能模型从一开始就具有我们想要的特征的分子，而不是仅在生成肽后对其进行评估。”

不断扩大的肽数据领域

研究人员还设计了 PeptiVerse 以保持增长。 随着更多的肽数据可用，该平台可以使用新的数据集、改进的模型和其他特性进行更新。 这很重要，因为某些肽特性仍然比其他特性更难预测，很大程度上是因为可用于训练有关它们的模型的实验数据较少。

更多的测量可以帮助 PeptiVerse 改进现有的预测并添加新的预测，例如肽是否可能激活或阻断受体，从而激活或关闭特定的生化途径。 “PeptiVerse 并不意味着已经完成，”Chatterjee 说道。 “社区贡献的数据越多，这些模型就会变得越好。”

由于 PeptiVerse 是开源的，研究人员可以根据自己的目的对其进行调整。 学术实验室可以使用现有平台，贡献数据或构建新的预测器。 开发肽疗法的公司还可以使用该框架与自己的内部数据集，创建适合他们试图开发的分子类型的 PeptiVerse 版本。

“可能的肽宇宙太大了，任何一个实验室都无法独自绘制地图，”查特吉说。 “我们构建了 PeptiVerse，以便其他研究人员可以帮助扩展地图，添加新的数据和模型，从而加速寻找新的、更好的肽药物。”

出版详情

一诺张等人，PeptiVerse：治疗性肽特性预测的统一平台，自然通讯（2026）。 DOI：10.1038/s41467-026-74167-w
